evidence_gaps: No direct analytics or search log data provided
Homepage lacks explicit user journey prioritization signals (e.g., primary CTA hierarchy)
No behavioral data on completion rates for complaint filing

task_longlist.task_statement: File a discrimination complaint with OCR

evidence.note: Prominent link to 'Filing a Complaint with OCR'
Detailed instructions for filing civil rights, HIPAA, and other complaints

task_longlist.rationale: Core user need and primary service; clearly emphasized across homepage and subpages.

task_longlist.task_statement: Understand my civil rights in healthcare

evidence.note: Explains protections against discrimination in health services

task_longlist.rationale: Fundamental awareness task for all audiences; directly tied to OCR mission. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

task_longlist.task_statement: Find HIPAA privacy rights and rules

evidence.note: Dedicated section on privacy rule and protected information

task_longlist.rationale: High-volume, high-impact topic spanning public and providers. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

task_longlist.task_statement: Check if discrimination applies to my situation

evidence.note: Lists protected classes and scenarios (race, disability, age, etc.)

task_longlist.rationale: Precursor to filing complaints; moderately harder to interpret due to legal framing. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

task_longlist.task_statement: Learn how to comply with civil rights laws

evidence.note: Guidance for providers on compliance obligations

task_longlist.rationale: Critical for providers but less clearly segmented from public content. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}
Sub-task of complaint filing but distinct high-demand use case. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

task_longlist.task_statement: Contact OCR for help or questions

evidence.note: Phone, email, and regional office contacts provided

task_longlist.rationale: Important fallback task but not primary entry point. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}
Critical for LEP users; highly visible but narrower audience. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}
Important but more niche compared to general civil rights and HIPAA. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}
Low task criticality relative to action-oriented needs. :contentReference[oaicite:8]{index=8}

evidence.note: Links to niche areas like HIV/AIDS and child welfare

task_longlist.rationale: Fragmented and niche content compared to core tasks. :contentReference[oaicite:9]{index=9}

recommended_survey.instructions: Please select the 5 tasks that are most important to you when visiting this website.
task_list_for_voting: File a discrimination complaint with OCR
Understand my civil rights in healthcare
Find HIPAA privacy rights and rules
Check if discrimination applies to my situation
Learn how to comply with civil rights laws
Contact OCR for help or questions

next_steps: Validate top tasks through a forced-choice survey with real users
Analyze search logs to confirm high-frequency intents (complaints, HIPAA, rights)
Audit complaint filing journey for drop-offs and friction
Segment navigation for public vs provider tasks
Prioritize homepage redesign around top 3–5 tasks
summary: The dominant user needs are filing complaints, understanding civil rights, and accessing HIPAA privacy information. These tasks are high-frequency and high-impact but not always clearly segmented by audience. A key risk is mixing public and provider content, making it harder for users to quickly determine next steps. Complaint filing is well supported but may require clearer pathways and guidance. Validation should focus on confirming task priority and improving findability for eligibility and compliance tasks.
